meta.title: Grammar Mastery: Subtle Distinctions & Edge Cases
meta.title_ko: 문법 숙달: 미묘한 구별과 경계 사례
meta.description: Address the subtlest grammatical distinctions that even advanced learners frequently confuse.
meta.description_ko: 심화 학습자들도 자주 혼동하는 가장 미묘한 문법적 구별을 다룹니다.

explanation.en: BrE: collective nouns as plural (treating members as individuals). AmE: singular (treating as a unit).
explanation.ko: 영국 영어: 집합 명사 복수형(구성원을 개인으로 취급). 미국 영어: 단수형(하나의 단위로 취급).

questions.prompt: 'The committee have decided' (BrE) vs 'The committee has decided' (AmE) — 이 차이의 이유는?
choices: 집합 명사는 영국 영어에서 구성원에 초점 → 복수, 미국 영어에서 하나의 단위 → 단수

explanation.en: 'Glad that + indicative' is standard. The subjunctive after 'glad' is rare in modern English.
explanation.ko: 'glad that + 직설법'이 표준. 현대 영어에서 'glad' 뒤의 가정법은 드물다.

questions.prompt: 'I am _____ that the policy has been enacted.' — formal subjunctive after expressions of emotion

explanation.en: 'None' can be singular or plural. Singular ('was') is traditionally preferred; plural is also widely accepted.
explanation.ko: 'none'은 단수나 복수 모두 가능. 단수('was')가 전통적으로 선호; 복수도 널리 수용됨.

questions.prompt: 'None of the students _____ prepared.' — 어느 것이 더 정확한가?
choices: was/were 둘 다 가능하나 'was'가 더 격식체

explanation.en: 'It is essential that + subject + bare infinitive' = present subjunctive (formal).
explanation.ko: 'It is essential that + 주어 + 동사 원형' = 현재 가정법(격식체).

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: It is essential that every member of the team be fully briefed before the negotiations commence.

explanation.en: After 'than' in formal English: 'than I thought' (subject of elliptical clause, not object).
explanation.ko: 격식 영어에서 'than' 뒤: 'than I thought'(생략절의 주어, 목적어 아님).

questions.prompt: 'She is taller than _____ thought.' — 어느 것이 문법적으로 더 정확한가?

explanation.en: 'However + adjective + subject + be' = formal concessive construction. 'However counterintuitive they be'.
explanation.ko: 'however + 형용사 + 주어 + be' = 격식체 양보 구조.

questions.prompt: The findings, _____ be they ever so counterintuitive, demand our attention.

explanation.en: Formally: 'To whom should I give this?' But 'Whom should I give this to?' is also acceptable; 'who' is informal.
explanation.ko: 격식체: 'To whom should I give this?' 그러나 'Whom should I give this to?'도 수용 가능; 'who'는 비격식.

questions.prompt: 'Whom should I give this to?' vs 'Who should I give this to?' — 어느 것이 격식체에서 올바른가?
choices: Whom should I give this to? (격식체에서 목적격 사용)
Who should I give this to? (항상 옳다)
'To whom should I give this?' 만이 올바르다

explanation.en: Inverted 3rd conditional: 'Had I known... I would have acted' (result clause).
explanation.ko: 도치된 3형 조건문: 'Had I known... I would have acted'(결과절).

questions.prompt: 'Had I known then what I know now, I _____ acted differently.' — 빈칸에 알맞은 것은?

explanation.en: 'Data' is technically plural (datum → data) but singular use is increasingly common informally.
explanation.ko: 'data'는 기술적으로 복수(datum → data)이지만 비격식에서 단수 사용이 점점 일반화됨.

questions.prompt: 'The data is clear' vs 'The data are clear' — 현대 영어에서?
choices: 학술 맥락에서는 'are'가 더 정확하지만 'is'도 점점 수용되는 추세

explanation.en: 'I wish + past subjunctive': 'were' for all persons in formal English.
explanation.ko: 'I wish + 과거 가정법': 격식 영어에서 모든 인칭에 'were'.

questions.prompt: 'I wish he _____ here now.' — 현재 가정에 올바른 동사형은?

explanation.en: 'Between you and me' — 'between' requires object pronouns. 'Between you and I' is a hypercorrection.
explanation.ko: 'between you and me' — 'between'은 목적격 대명사가 필요. 'between you and I'는 과잉 교정.

questions.prompt: 다음 중 완전히 옳은 문장은?
choices: Between you and I, this is problematic.
Everyone must submit their form, mustn't they?
Between you and me, this is problematic.
The reason is because he left early.

explanation.en: 'The number of + plural noun + singular verb'. 'Far greater than anticipated' = elliptical comparison.
explanation.ko: 'The number of + 복수 명사 + 단수 동사'. 'Far greater than anticipated' = 생략 비교.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: The number of applications received was far greater than anticipated, suggesting a pent-up demand that had gone unrecognised.

explanation.en: After 'be', the subject complement takes nominative case: 'It was he' (not 'him') in strict formal English.
explanation.ko: 'be' 뒤에서 주어 보어는 주격을 취한다: 엄격한 격식 영어에서 'It was he'('him' 아님).

questions.prompt: 'It was he who discovered the error' — why not 'him'?
choices: Because it is a subject complement after 'be' (nominative case)

explanation.en: 'Lend' = verb (preferred in BrE). 'Loan' = noun, but also used as verb in AmE.
explanation.ko: 'lend' = 동사(영국 영어에서 선호). 'loan' = 명사이지만 미국 영어에서 동사로도 사용됨.

questions.prompt: 'lend' vs 'loan' — 어떤 차이가 있나요?
choices: 'lend'는 동사(BrE에서 선호), 'loan'은 명사이지만 동사로도 사용(AmE에서 흔함)

explanation.en: 'Despite + noun/gerund phrase': 'despite overwhelming evidence' (adjective modifying the noun).
explanation.ko: 'despite + 명사/동명사구': 'despite overwhelming evidence'(명사를 수식하는 형용사).

questions.prompt: 'Despite _____ the evidence, the jury acquitted the defendant.' — 빈칸에 알맞은 것은?

explanation.en: 'The reason is because' = non-standard. Correct: 'The reason is that'. 'Because' introduces cause, not reason.
explanation.ko: 'The reason is because' = 비표준. 올바른 형태: 'The reason is that'.

questions.prompt: 문법적으로 틀린 문장은?
choices: The reason why he failed is because he didn't prepare.
He succeeded because he was well prepared.
The reason he failed was that he didn't prepare.
He failed because of poor preparation.

explanation.en: 'A certain + adjective' (intensifying/emphasising): 'a certain rare authority' = a notably rare kind.
explanation.ko: 'a certain + 형용사'(강조): 'a certain rare authority' = 특히 드문 종류의.

questions.prompt: 'She spoke with a _____ rare authority, her voice carrying the weight of someone who had witnessed these events firsthand.'

explanation.en: 'Together with' is a parenthetical phrase; the subject is 'the professor' (singular) → 'has'.
explanation.ko: 'together with'는 삽입구; 주어는 'the professor'(단수) → 'has'.

questions.prompt: 'The professor, together with her colleagues, _____ published the findings.' — 올바른 동사 형태는?

explanation.en: 'Not until + clause + inversion' = formal negative fronting with subject-verb inversion.
explanation.ko: 'not until + 절 + 도치' = 주어-동사 도치를 사용한 격식체 부정 전치.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Not until the full extent of the deception was revealed did the gravity of the situation become apparent.

explanation.en: 'Each' = singular → 'has been', not 'have been'. Subject-verb agreement.
explanation.ko: 'each' = 단수 → 'has been', 'have been' 아님. 주어-동사 일치.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'Each of the proposals have been carefully evaluated by the committee.'

explanation.en: 'However + adjective + subject + may be' = concessive clause.
explanation.ko: 'however + 형용사 + 주어 + may be' = 양보절.

questions.prompt: 'The solution, _____ complex it may be, is preferable to the alternative.' — 빈칸에 알맞은 것은?

explanation.en: 'Whom' = object case. If you can substitute 'him/her', use 'whom'.
explanation.ko: 'whom' = 목적격. 'him/her'로 대체 가능하면 'whom'을 사용.

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: Should I use "who" or "whom"? B: Use "whom" when it functions as the _____ of a verb or preposition — a fast test is to replace it with "him/her".'

explanation.en: Key C2-level grammar rules and their correct applications.
explanation.ko: 주요 C2 수준 문법 규칙과 올바른 적용.

questions.prompt: 문법 규칙과 올바른 형태를 연결하세요.
